questions.question_text: How many hours of pre-license education must a salesperson candidate complete in North Dakota?
questions.explanation: Since January 1, 2021, North Dakota requires 90 hours of NDREC-approved pre-license education for salesperson candidates.

questions.question_text: A salesperson completed pre-license education on June 1, 2024 but has not yet applied for a license. What is the deadline to apply?
questions.explanation: North Dakota requires applicants to apply for licensure within 2 years of completing pre-license education. After that period, the education is no longer valid for application purposes.

questions.question_text: A candidate wants to upgrade from salesperson to broker. How many additional education hours are required beyond the salesperson pre-license?
questions.explanation: North Dakota broker candidates must complete 60 additional hours of NDREC-approved education beyond the 90-hour salesperson pre-license requirement.

questions.question_text: A candidate completed a pre-license course from a school not approved by the NDREC. Can he use these hours toward his application?
distractors: Yes, if the school is nationally accredited
Yes, if the course covered the same topics
Yes, with a letter from the school
questions.explanation: North Dakota requires all pre-license education to be completed through NDREC-approved providers. Courses from unapproved schools do not count toward the licensing requirement.

questions.question_text: Before 2021, the pre-license education requirement for salesperson candidates was lower. What is the current requirement since January 2021?
questions.explanation: Effective January 1, 2021, North Dakota increased the salesperson pre-license requirement to 90 hours of NDREC-approved education.

questions.question_text: How many continuing education hours must a North Dakota licensee complete annually?
questions.explanation: North Dakota licensees must complete 12 hours of continuing education per year: 6 mandatory hours and 6 elective hours, during the cycle running November 16 through November 15.

questions.question_text: A licensee earned her license on August 1, 2025. Her first renewal is December 31, 2025. How many CE hours must she complete for this renewal?
questions.correct_answer: She is exempt for her first renewal
questions.explanation: First-year licensees in North Dakota are exempt from continuing education requirements for their first renewal cycle.

questions.question_text: For the 2026 CE cycle, which mandatory topics must North Dakota licensees complete?
questions.correct_answer: 3 hours DEI and 3 hours vacant property
distractors: 3 hours fair housing and 3 hours contracts
questions.explanation: For the 2026 continuing education cycle, the NDREC mandates 3 hours of DEI (Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion) and 3 hours covering vacant property and scams, totaling the required 6 mandatory hours.

questions.question_text: The North Dakota CE cycle runs from which date to which date?
questions.explanation: The North Dakota continuing education cycle runs from November 16 through November 15 of the following year, aligning with the November 15 renewal deadline.

questions.question_text: A licensee completed 6 mandatory CE hours but only 4 elective hours by November 15. What is the consequence?
questions.correct_answer: The licensee has not met CE requirements
distractors: The 2 missing hours carry to next cycle
The licensee receives a partial credit
questions.explanation: North Dakota requires 12 total CE hours: 6 mandatory and 6 elective. Completing only 10 hours means the licensee has not met the annual CE requirement, which can affect license renewal.

questions.question_text: All North Dakota real estate licenses expire on which date?
questions.explanation: All North Dakota real estate licenses expire on December 31 of each year, regardless of when the license was originally issued.

questions.question_text: A broker submits her renewal application on November 20, five days past the deadline. What consequence does she face?
distractors: No penalty if received by December 31
Mandatory retaking of the licensing exam
questions.explanation: The renewal deadline is November 15. Applications received after that date but before cancellation are subject to late fees ranging from $100 to $300. There is no grace period for late renewals.

questions.question_text: A salesperson fails to renew by the deadline. On January 1, the license becomes inactive. What happens if the licensee still has not renewed by January 15?
distractors: The licensee must retake the state exam only
questions.explanation: Licenses that are not renewed become inactive on January 1. If the licensee still has not renewed by January 15, the license is cancelled entirely.

questions.question_text: What is the annual renewal fee for a North Dakota salesperson license?
questions.explanation: The annual renewal fee is $125 for salespersons and $150 for brokers. These fees must be submitted by the November 15 renewal deadline.

questions.question_text: Is there a grace period after the November 15 renewal deadline in North Dakota?
questions.correct_answer: No, there is no grace period
questions.explanation: North Dakota does not provide a grace period for late renewals. Applications received after November 15 are considered late and subject to fees of $100 to $300.
